metadata.title: Microblock rotations and fault coupling in SE Asia triple junction (Sulawesi, Indonesia) from GPS and earthquake slip vector data
metadata.study_area: Sulawesi and surroundings, SE Asia triple junction (Australian, Philippine, Sunda plates)
metadata.method: GPS block modelling + earthquake slip vectors; DEFNODE rigid-block elastic coupling inversion; ~30+ campaign GPS sites + 6 continuous stations + 25 Palu/Gorontalo transect sites (1992-2004)
conclusions: Sulawesi deformation described by 5 rapidly rotating microblocks: Makassar (ACW -1.4°/Myr vs Sunda), North Sula (CW +2.6°/Myr), Manado (CW +3.2°/Myr), East Sulawesi (ACW -2.2°/Myr), Banda Sea (ACW -1.8°/Myr)
Palu-Koro Fault (PKF): 41-44 mm/yr total; 39 mm/yr left-lateral + 11-14 mm/yr extension; transtensive pull-apart at Palu; possibly 3-4 parallel strands each locked to 1-15 km depth
Gorontalo Fault: 11 mm/yr right-lateral (dextral transtension), locked to 10 km depth; separates North Sula Block from Manado Block
Lawanopo Fault: ~23-25 mm/yr left-lateral; poorly known whether PKF continues as Matano or Lawanopo fault to the south
Minahassa Trench: currently uncoupled (j≈0) due to postseismic relaxation from January 1996 Mw 7.9 earthquake; full locking expected to resume
Block rotation in N. Sulawesi driven by Borneo collision resistance (W) + oceanic trench suction (N); clockwise rotation of North Sula Block instead of mountain building
East Sulawesi Trench accommodates ~15 mm/yr; Makassar Trench accommodates 4-13 mm/yr (locked)

properties.name: Palu-Koro Fault
properties.slip_rate_description: GPS interseismic deficit; total block motion North Sula vs Makassar
properties.azimuth_note: Azimuth varies from -21° (south) to +7° (north) along fault strike
properties.coupling_model: Model 2 (GPS + slip vectors); variable coupling allowed along strike
properties.pull_apart: Palu basin; possible 3-4 parallel strands locked to 1-15 km depth; transtensive motifs seen in morphology (triangular facets)
properties.paleoslip_note: 35±8 mm/yr from stream and fan offsets (Bellier et al. 2001); GPS agrees at high end
properties.context: Boundary between Makassar Block (SW) and North Sula Block (NE); western continuation of Sula-Sorong/Matano fault system; connects to Minahassa Trench (N) and Matano/Lawanopo fault (S)

properties.name: Gorontalo Fault
properties.context: Boundary between North Sula Block (W) and Manado Block (E); bisects northern arm of Sulawesi; connects to Minahassa Trench at eastern end. Dextral transtension with 11 mm/yr; accumulates interseismic elastic strain.

properties.name: Lawanopo Fault
properties.context: Southern boundary of East Sulawesi Block; western continuation of Sula-Sorong Fault into Sulawesi; parallel to and south of Matano Fault. It is unclear whether PKF connects southward to Matano or Lawanopo fault. ~25 mm/yr left-lateral interseismic deficit.

properties.name: Tomini Gulf Fault Zone
properties.context: Northern boundary of East Sulawesi Block inferred from GPS velocities; zone of frequent seismicity beneath Gulf of Tomini (roughly E-W). Right-lateral ~24 mm/yr. Inferred structure, not confirmed from geology alone.

properties.name: Euler Pole — Makassar Block / Sunda
properties.interpretation: The southwestern part of Sulawesi (Makassar Block) rotates anticlockwise at -1.4°/Myr relative to the Sunda Plate about a pole near its southwestern tip. Makassar Trench to the west accommodates convergence; East Sulawesi Trench bounds it to the SE.

properties.name: Euler Pole — North Sula Block / Sunda
properties.interpretation: North Sula Block rotates clockwise at +2.6°/Myr relative to Sunda; pole near NE tip. Block moves toward NNW and is pinched between PKF/Lawanopo (S), Minahassa Trench (N), Gorontalo Fault (E). Clockwise rotation preferred over mountain building due to low-stress boundary (oceanic crust) to the north.

properties.name: Euler Pole — Manado Block / Sunda
properties.interpretation: Eastern part of northern arm (Manado Block) rotates clockwise at +3.2°/Myr relative to Sunda, faster than North Sula Block. Separated from North Sula Block by Gorontalo Fault (11 mm/yr right-lateral).

properties.name: Euler Pole — East Sulawesi Block / Sunda
properties.interpretation: East Sulawesi Block (SE arm area) rotates anticlockwise at -2.2°/Myr. GPS sites AMPA and LUWU show significant westward motion relative to North Sula Block. Boundaries are poorly defined: Lawanopo Fault (S), Matano Fault (N?), Tomini Gulf fault zone (N). Independent from Makassar Block at 99% confidence.
